question: Can Metformin be safely prescribed to a 68-year-old with Type 2 Diabetes and Stage 4 chronic kidney disease (eGFR 22)?
reference_answer: No. Metformin is contraindicated at eGFR <30 due to lactic acidosis risk. The ADA recommends switching to a DPP-4 inhibitor (e.g., linagliptin) or GLP-1 receptor agonist; insulin remains an option. Reference: ADA Standards of Care 2024.
